channel.title: AI Agents on DevOpsTales
channel.description: Recent content in AI Agents on DevOpsTales

item.title: GSD – Get Shit Done: AI-Powered Spec-Driven Development Pipeline
item.description: If you’ve spent any time working with AI coding assistants, you’ve probably encountered context rot — that moment when your AI session loses track of what it’s doing, starts hallucinating, or produces code that doesn’t match the original plan. The more complex the task, the faster things spiral.

item.title: Hermes Agent vs OpenClaw: The Ultimate AI Agent Showdown
item.description: The AI agent landscape in 2026 is crowded, noisy, and full of promises. Two names keep surfacing in developer conversations: OpenClaw and Hermes Agent. Both claim to be the autonomous assistant that finally bridges the gap between AI reasoning and real-world execution. Both are open-source. Both run locally. Both connect to messaging platforms.

item.title: Parallel Agent Development: The Future of AI-Powered Software Engineering
item.description: For years, AI coding assistants worked the way a single intern would: one task at a time, one conversation at a time, one file at a time. You asked a question, waited for an answer, then asked the next one. Sequential. Linear. Slow. In 2026, that model is dead.
